channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: Tyreek Hill said he has “no power” in his left leg.
item.description: Hill suffered a dislocated knee, torn ACL and LCL, and other ligament damage in Week 4 of the 2025 season. He posted a video of himself working out and said he didn’t have a timeline to return, but added that the timeline is “looking real good right now.” Still, the fact that he has no power in his recovering leg speaks louder than his words. Hill is currently a free agent and is likely several months away from playing if things go well. It’s also unlikely we know where he is playing until deep into the season. Fantasy managers, in turn, shouldn’t worry about him in summer drafts.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: NBC Houston’s Aaron Wilson reports Joe Mixon told former teammates from the Bengals “his career is over.”
item.description: Wilson did note that a source close to Mixon told him that there is “some hope”, though it doesn’t sound awfully likely that Mixon ever steps on a football field again. Mixon was unable to play last year after injuring his foot and undergoing surgery on his own. The Texans seemed to be in the dark throughout the process and were never able to give a clear answer on when or if Mixon would return. They went on to cut him after he missed the entire 2025 season. Per Wilson, Mixon needed another surgery this offseason. Even if he does somehow return to action, there has been no indication that it will happen this year.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Cardinals TE Travis Vokolek retired after three seasons in the NFL.
item.description: Vokolek announces his retirement on social media Wednesday, noting a neck injury as the cause. The Cardinals tight end was carted off in Week 6 of the 2025 season. The neck injury was serious enough for doctors to advise Vokolek to “medically retire from the NFL.” Coming out of Nebraska as an undrafted free agent in 2023, Vokolek signed with the Ravens’ practice squad. The Cardinals signed him from there and he appeared in eight games over the course of three seasons in Arizona. Primarily a special teamer and blocker, Vokolek’s career ends at 28-years-old.-Aditya Fuldeore

item.title: NFL Network’s Mike Garafolo reports QB Jimmy Garoppolo will play football this season for “the right situation.”
item.description: Garafolo adds that Garoppolo “has not decided to retire” and will continue to play. Nothing is imminent and the “right situation” may take an injury to a starter around the league. The 34-year-old had contemplated retirement earlier in the offseason but instead is open to continuing his playing career. Garoppolo is a free agent to keep an eye on as training camp battles and injuries start to unfold.-Aditya Fuldeore

item.title: Seahawks S Nick Emmanwori underwent surgery on his right ankle and is expected to be back for Week 1.
item.description: Emmanwori recently landed on the PUP list, now revealed to be due to a recent right ankle surgery. He dealt with ankle issues all of last season but played through it in the Seahawks’ Super Bowl victory. ESPN’s Brady Henderson reports Emmanwori is expected to miss some time in training camp; the 22-year-old safety is expected to be back for Week 1.-Aditya Fuldeore

item.title: NFL Network’s Mike Garafolo reports Cardinals QB Jacoby Brissett reported to training camp while awaiting a new contract.
item.description: Brissett and the Cardinals have had discussions on a new contract that will pay him like a starter. The 33-year-old was Arizona’s starting quarterback for the back half of last season and he now wants a pay bump from his $1.5 million in guarantees this season. The two sides have yet to reach a deal and Brissett still very much desires the new deal despite him reporting to training camp. It’s possible he will be a camp “hold-in” until the Cardinals get him the desired contract.-Aditya Fuldeore

item.title: Raiders signed fourth-round rookie Jermod McCoy to a four-year contract.
item.description: McCoy slid to the fourth round of the draft because of long-term health concerns surrounding his knee. He missed the 2025 season with a torn ACL and there were reports late in the draft cycle that he would need a second surgery, which would have sidelined him for the entirety of his rookie season. That hasn’t happened and McCoy managed to get some limited reps during OTAs and training camp. McCoy participated in walkthroughs and individual drills before sitting out team drills. All signs point toward him being available at some point during training camp and playing in some capacity this season.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Cardinals guard Isaiah Adams is facing charges for possessing a weapon by crime, possessing a restricted or prohibited firearm without a license, possessing a loaded firearm, and possessing a prohibited device.
item.description: Adams turned himself in to Toronto police on Sunday. Per ESPN’s Josh Weinfuss, he is still expected to report to training camp. Adams was expected to compete for a starting job at right guard this summer, but the off-field concerns could see him shuffled to the bench for the start of the season. A 2024 third-round pick, Adams made 11 starts in 2025 and five starts as a rookie.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: The Athletic’s Zack Rosenblatt believes Isaiah Davis is the “early favorite” for the Jets’ backup running back job.
item.description: Rosenblatt led his breakdown by noting that Davis and Braelon Allen will both get snaps, so there isn’t likely to be a clear winner behind Breece Hall. Allen missed most of the 2025 season with a knee injury. In his absence, the Jets leaned on Davis as a pass-catcher to give Hall the occasional breather. He was targeted 28 times and brought down 21 of them for 186 yards. Davis has also been effective between the tackles, averaging 5.6 yards per carry through two seasons compared to 3.7 for Allen. The Jets’ backup job could have some appeal in deeper fantasy leagues, but it sounds like fantasy managers will want to get more info from training camp before placing their bets.-Kyle Dvorchak

item.title: Seahawks placed Zach Charbonnet (knee) on the active/PUP list.
item.description: Charbonnet is still recovering from the torn ACL he suffered in the Divisional Round last year. He can be activated from the PUP list at any time, though the most likely outcome is that he misses all of camp and is then moved to the reserve/PUP list. That would require him to miss at least the first four games of the season. With Kenneth Walker gone, the Seahawks will rely on first-round rookie Jadarian Price to start the year. George Holani and Emmanuel Wilson could also mix in for some touches, with Holani likely operating as the primary option on passing downs.-Kyle Dvorchak
